UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores personal information, health goals, and dietary preferences for a user to personalize the app experience.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

healthGoals.description: An array of strings describing the user's health objectives (e.g., 'weight loss', 'muscle gain').

dietPreferences.description: An array of strings describing the user's dietary choices (e.g., 'vegetarian', 'low carb').

healthConcerns.description: An array of strings describing specific health conditions (e.g., 'diabetes', 'high blood pressure').

allergies.description: An array of strings listing specific food allergies (e.g., 'peanuts', 'dairy').

foodsToAvoid.description: An array of strings listing general foods the user wishes to avoid.

preferredCuisine.description: An array of strings describing preferred cuisine types (e.g., 'Italian', 'Mexican', 'Asian').

householdSize.description: The number of people in the user's household.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Represents a single ingredient tracked by the user in their fridge, freezer, or pantry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this inventory item belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N InventoryItem)

name.description: The common name of the ingredient (e.g., 'milk', 'chicken breast').

category.description: The categorization of the ingredient (e.g., 'vegetables', 'fruits', 'meat', 'dairy', 'pantry items').

quantity.description: The amount of the ingredient available.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the quantity (e.g., 'grams', 'units', 'liters', 'oz').

expirationDate.description: The date when the ingredient is expected to expire.

notes.description: Any additional notes about the ingredient (optional).

location.description: The storage location of the ingredient (e.g., 'fridge', 'freezer', 'pantry').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory item was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory item was last updated.

Recipe.title: Recipe
Recipe.description: Represents a single recipe, which can be AI-generated or saved by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recipe entity.

description.description: A short description or summary of the recipe.

whyItMatchesHealthNeeds.description: An explanation generated by AI on why this recipe aligns with the user's health profile.

prepTimeMinutes.description: Estimated preparation time for the recipe in minutes.

cookTimeMinutes.description: Estimated cooking time for the recipe in minutes.

difficultyLevel.description: The skill level required to cook the recipe (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

servings.description: The number of servings the recipe yields.

instructions.description: An ordered array of strings, each representing a step in the cooking process.

estimatedCalories.description: Estimated total calories per serving or per recipe.

estimatedCarbs.description: Estimated total carbohydrates per serving or per recipe.

estimatedProtein.description: Estimated total protein per serving or per recipe.

estimatedFat.description: Estimated total fat per serving or per recipe.

healthNotes.description: An array of strings indicating specific health attributes (e.g., 'diabetic-friendly', 'low sodium', 'high protein', 'avoid due to allergy').

substitutionSuggestions.description: An array of strings suggesting alternative ingredients or cooking methods (optional).

cuisineType.description: The primary cuisine category of the recipe (e.g., 'Italian', 'Mexican').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe was created or generated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe was last updated.

RecipeIngredient.title: RecipeIngredient
RecipeIngredient.description: Describes a specific ingredient requirement for a Recipe.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecipeIngredient entity.

recipeId.description: Reference to the Recipe this ingredient belongs to. (Relationship: Recipe 1:N RecipeIngredient)

name.description: The name of the ingredient required (e.g., 'flour', 'chicken breast').

quantity.description: The required quantity of the ingredient.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the required quantity (e.g., 'cups', 'grams', 'pieces').

notes.description: Specific preparation notes for the ingredient (e.g., 'diced', 'freshly ground').

isOptional.description: Indicates if this ingredient is optional for the recipe.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe ingredient entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe ingredient entry was last updated.

ShoppingListItem.title: ShoppingListItem
ShoppingListItem.description: Represents an item on a user's shopping list, possibly generated from a recipe or added manually.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShoppingListItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this shopping list item belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ShoppingListItem)

itemName.description: The name of the item to be bought.

quantity.description: The amount of the item needed.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the quantity (e.g., 'grams', 'units', 'liters', 'bunch').

isBought.description: Boolean indicating if the item has been purchased.

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the shopping list.

boughtAt.description: Timestamp when the item was marked as bought (optional).

sourceRecipeId.description: Reference to the Recipe that generated this shopping list item (optional). (Relationship: Recipe 1:N ShoppingListItem)

notes.description: Any additional notes for the shopping item (e.g., 'organic', 'brand X').

MealPlanEntry.title: MealPlanEntry
MealPlanEntry.description: Represents a recipe planned by the user for a specific meal on a given date in their weekly meal planner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MealPlanEntry entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this meal plan entry belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MealPlanEntry)

recipeId.description: Reference to the Recipe planned for this entry. (Relationship: Recipe 1:N MealPlanEntry)

planDate.description: The specific date for which the meal is planned (YYYY-MM-DD).

servingsPlanned.description: The number of servings planned for this meal, allowing user adjustments.

isCooked.description: Boolean indicating if the meal has been cooked.

cookedAt.description: Timestamp when the meal was marked as cooked (optional).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the meal plan entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the meal plan entry was last updated.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Stores notifications for a user, such as expiring ingredients, low stock, or meal plan reminders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom the notification is intended. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Notification)

type.description: The category or type of notification (e.g., 'expiring_ingredient', 'low_stock', 'meal_plan_reminder', 'recipe_suggestion').

message.description: The content of the notification message.

relatedEntityId.description: Optional reference to an associated entity (e.g., InventoryItem ID for expiring ingredient, Recipe ID for recipe suggestion). This can be null. (Relationship: various 1:1 Notification)

isRead.description: Boolean indicating if the user has read the notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was generated.

scheduledSendTime.description: The intended time for the notification to be sent, if scheduled for the future.

UserRecipeInteraction.title: UserRecipeInteraction
UserRecipeInteraction.description: Tracks a user's interactions with recipes, such as viewing, saving, cooking, or rating them, forming a comprehensive recipe history.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserRecipeInteraction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile interacting with the recipe. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserRecipeInteraction)

recipeId.description: Reference to the Recipe being interacted with. (Relationship: Recipe 1:N UserRecipeInteraction)

rating.description: Optional rating given by the user (1-5), applicable if interactionType is 'rated'.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments from the user about this interaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user recipe interaction was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user recipe interaction was last updated.

definition.description: Stores personal information, health goals, and dietary preferences for a user. The document ID is the user's Firebase Authentication UID. Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`.

Represents a single ingredient tracked by the user. Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence and query flexibility.
Represents a single recipe, which can be AI-generated or saved by a user. These are global recipes, intended to be broadly accessible to authenticated users.

Describes a specific ingredient requirement for a Recipe. Access is dependent on the parent 'Recipe' document's accessibility. No additional denormalized authorization fields are required as 'Recipes' are broadly accessible.

Represents an item on a user's shopping list. Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence and query flexibility.

Represents a recipe planned by the user for a specific meal on a given date. Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence and query flexibility.
Stores notifications for a user. Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence and query flexibility.

Tracks a user's interactions with recipes (viewing, saving, cooking, rating). Authorization is path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence and query flexibility.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

Authorization Independence is achieved primarily through path-based ownership for all user-specific data. For entities like `UserProfile`, `InventoryItem`, `ShoppingListItem`, `MealPlanEntry`, `Notification`, and `UserRecipeInteraction`, the owning `userId` is explicitly part of the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/inventoryItems/{inventoryItemId}`). This means that Firestore security rules can authorize requests by simply comparing `request.auth.uid` to the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. The `userId` field is also denormalized into each of these user-owned documents, providing an additional, redundant check for rules and simplifying client-side queries. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document ownership, which is critical for supporting atomic operations and preventing complex, hard-to-debug rules.

Structural Segregation is implemented by separating global, broadly accessible `Recipe` data into its own top-level collection (`/recipes/{recipeId}`). User-specific interactions and data are strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}/...` paths. This ensures that documents with different security postures (e.g., public/shared recipes vs. private user inventory) do not reside in the same collection, greatly simplifying security rules. For example, all documents within `/users/{userId}/inventoryItems` share the same ownership-based security requirements.

QAPs (Query/Access Patterns) are fully supported by this structure. Secure `list` operations are straightforward because all user-specific collections are directly owned by the `request.auth.uid` via the path wildcard. For instance, a user can only list `InventoryItem`s under their specific `/users/{userId}/inventoryItems` path, where `{userId}` matches their `auth.uid`. Similarly, listing `Recipe`s from the `/recipes` collection is secure if all authenticated users are permitted to read them, as no `get()` call to a parent is needed. Nested collections like `RecipeIngredient` inherit the access posture of their parent `Recipe`, simplifying their rules as well. This design makes authorization intent explicit and minimizes rule complexity, fulfilling the clarity of intent principle.
